x-sap-shortText: Allows you to interact, as a data provider, with assets from the SAP Datasphere Data Marketplace.

info.title: Data Sharing Cockpit
info.description:
The Data Sharing Cockpit APIs allow you to work within the data sharing cockpit of the Data Marketplace. The Data Sharing Cockpit is the central entry point to manage all data provider-specific tasks:

  • Maintain your data provider profile.
  • Create and edit data products.
  • Manage licenses.
  • Create and publish releases.
  • Manage contexts.

get.summary: Returns a list of data providers.
get.description: Returns a list of all the data providers that the current user has access to. To get access to a data provider, the user needs to be member of the data provider profile. If a contentAggregatorID is given as parameter, all data provider profiles that are managed by that content aggregator are returned.

post.summary: Creates a new data provider.
post.description: Creates a new data provider or a new managed data provider (managed by a content aggregator) based on a configuration following the data provider definition json format. Specify the .json in the request body. To create a managed data provider, specify the contentAggregatorID in the request body. Managed providers can only be created if you are a member of the assigned content aggregator profile.

Location.description: returns a path to the created resource that can be used in GET,PUT,PATCH,DELETE calls.

get.summary: Lists the properties of a data provider.
get.description: Lists all the properties of a certain data provider after specifying its UUID or its content aggregator UUID.

put.summary: Overwrites the properties of a data provider.
put.description: Overwrites all the properties of a data provider after specifying its UUID or its content aggregator UUID.

patch.summary: Updates selected properties of a data provider.
patch.description: Updates only selected properties of the specified data provider which are defined in the provided data provider definition json.

get.summary: Returns a list of all the existing activation keys for a specified data provider.

post.summary: Generates activation keys for a specified data provider.
Deletes all the activation keys for a specified data provider.

example: Failed to delete Provider key: nmbfc8e3-5790-4b12-970e-238ae51e9783 because invalid key

get.summary: Returns a list of data products.
get.description: Returns a list of all the existing data products assigned to your user. If no parameter is given, it returns all the data products of all the data provider the user has access to. If the given providerUUID represents a content aggregator, all products of all data provider belonging to the content aggregator are returned. If a providerUUID is given, all products of this data provider are returned.

post.summary: Creates a new data product.
post.description: Creates a new data product based on a configuration following the data product definition json format.

Location.description: returns a path to the created resource that can be used in GET,PUT,PATCH,DELETE calls.

get.summary: Lists the properties of a data product.
get.description: Lists all the properties of a single data product. To do so, you must specify the data product UUID.

put.summary: Overwrites properties of a data product.
put.description: Overwrites all the properties of a specified data product with the provided data from the data product definition json.

patch.summary: Updates selected properties of a data product.
patch.description: Updates only selected properties of a specified data product which are defined in the provided data product definition json.

delete.description: Deletes an existing data product. To do so, you must specify the data product UUID.

post.summary: Changes the lifecycle status of a data product.
post.description: Changes the lifecycle status of a data product. To do so, you must specify the data product UUID.

get.summary: Returns a list of data products for a data provider.
get.description: Returns a list of all the data products of a data provider that the user has access to.

post.summary: Creates a new data product for a data provider.
post.description: Creates a new data product for a specified data provider based on a configuration following the data product definition json format.

Location.description: returns a path to the created resource that can be used in GET,PUT,PATCH,DELETE calls.

get.summary: Lists the properties of a data product of a specified data provider.
get.description: Lists all the properties of a data product. To do so, you must specify the technical ID and data provider UUID or content aggregator UUID.

put.summary: Overwrites all the properties of a data product of a specified data provider.
put.description: Overwrites all the properties of a specified data product with the provided data in the data product definition json.

patch.summary: Updates properties of a data product of a specified data provider.
patch.description: Updates only selected properties of a specified data product which are defined in the provided data product definition json.

delete.summary: Deletes an existing data product of a specified data provider.

post.summary: Changes the lifecycle status of a data product of a specified data provider.
post.description: Changes the lifecycle status of a data product. To do so, you must specify the data product UUID.

get.description: Returns a simplified list of all the licenses available for your data provider profile.

post.summary: Creates a new license.
Deletes all the licenses from a specified data provider.

example: Failed to delete License key:b3bfc8e3-5790-4b12-970e-238ae51e9783 because invalid key

get.summary: Lists the properties of a license.
get.description: Lists all the properties of a specified license. To do so, you must specify the license UUID.

put.summary: Overwrites the properties of a license.
put.description: Overwrites all the properties of a specified license with the provided data in the license definition json.

patch.summary: Updates the properties of a license.
patch.description: Updates some properties of a specified license. To do so, you must specify the license UUID.

post.summary: Changes the lifecycle status of a license.
post.description: Changes the lifecycle status of a license from Draft to Active, or vice versa.

get.summary: Lists data products assigned to a license.
get.description: Returns a list of all the data products assigned to a specified license.

post.summary: Assigns data products to a license.
Unassigns all the data products from a specified license.

get.summary: Returns a list of all the existing activation keys for a specified license.

post.summary: Generates activation keys for a specified license.
Deletes all the activation keys from a specified license.

get.summary: Returns a list of all the releases of a specified data product.

post.summary: Creates a new release for a specified data product.

get.summary: Lists the properties of a release.
get.description: Lists all the properties of a specified release. To do so, you must specify the release UUID.

put.summary: Overwrites all the properties of a release.
put.description: Overwrites all the properties of a specified release with the provided data in the data product definition file.

patch.summary: Updates the properties of a release.
patch.description: Updates some properties of a specified release. To do so, you must specify the release UUID.

delete.summary: Deletes a release for a specified data product.

post.summary: Publishes a release to make data product updates available to the consumers.
Locks a certain release of a specified data product.
Unlocks a certain release of a specified data product.
Locks all the releases of a specified data product.
Unlocks all the releases of a specified data product.

get.summary: Returns a list of contexts for a specified data provider.
get.description: Returns a list of all the contexts of a data provider that the user has access to.

post.summary: Creates a new context for a data provider.
post.description: Creates a new context for a specified data provider based on a configuration following the context definition file format and stored as a .json file.

get.summary: Lists the properties of a context of a specified data provider.
get.description: Lists all the properties of a context. To do so, you must specify the context UUID and data provider UUID.

put.summary: Overwrites all the properties of a context of a specified data provider.
put.description: Overwrites all the properties of a specified context with the provided data in the context definition json.

patch.summary: Updates properties of a context of a specified data provider.
patch.description: Updates only selected properties of a specified context which are defined in the provided context definition file.

delete.summary: Deletes an existing context from a specified data provider.

post.summary: Changes the lifecycle status of a context of a specified data provider.
post.description: Changes the lifecycle status of a context. To do so, you must specify the context UUID.

post.summary: Ends membership of a context.
post.description: Leaves a context of a specified data provider":" only applicable if you don't own the data provider.

example: Failed to leave the Context: d064fd58-10d0-46dc-916d-3efbeaeb9882 because: invalidProviderMembership

post.summary: Creates membership of a context.
post.description: Joins a context of a specified data provider":" only applicable if you don't own the data provider.

example: successfully joined context xyz, using key 18cf16de-5dcc-4734-b25a-f493b2ad752f.

post.summary: Deletes all the contexts from a specified data provider.

example: Failed to delete context :b3bfc8e3-5790-4b12-970e-238ae51e9783 because invalid ID

get.summary: Returns a list of all the existing activation keys for a specified context.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Contains the member type. Either provider or consumer

post.summary: Generates activation keys for a specified context.
Deletes all the activation keys from a specified context.

example: Failed to delete Context key:d4bfc8e3-5790-4b12-970e-238ae51e9783 because invalid key

bearerAuth.description: To use this API, you must have an access token.

contentAggregatorID.description: UUID of the content aggregator for managed provider.

contentAggregatorProviderID.description: ID of the data provider in the system of the content aggregator. Only relevant for managed providers (contentAggregatorID is given).

description.example: Lorem Ipsum description of my Provider

contactEmail.description: eMail address that is used if a customer contacts the data provider

sapEmail.description: email address that is used by SAP to contact the data provider

shipments.description: Can be used to specify the shipment types that a Data Provider is offering. These shipment types are used if a customer filters offerings for a distinct shipment type. It is particularly important it the DataProvider has not products listed yet as only the shipment types given here are used for the search.

marketplaceVisibilities.description: Marketplace Visibilities of a Provider can be public, private or both

contentAggregatorID.description: UUID of the content aggregator for managed providers

dataProviderProductID.description: The unique ID of the given product in the system of the data provider

contentAggregatorProductID.description: The unique ID of the given product in the system of the content aggregator (if the corresponding data provider is managed by a content aggregator).

name.description: Must be unique within a data provider

providerUUID.description: The UUID of the data provider

description.example: Lorem Ipsum description of my Provider

space.description: Technical name of the space that contains the referenced data artifacts of the product. If Shipment Type is "Direct", this is a mandatory property. For all other Shipment Types the Source Space is optional. The user who calls the API has to have access to the space (has to be a space member).

pricingDescription.description: Mandatory for contract type “LicenseKey”, otherwise optional. Verbal description of the pricing model and/or metric and potentially also the price.

price.description: Mandatory for pricing model “OneTime”, otherwise optional. Gives the product price. The associated currency also has to be provided (priceCurrencyCode)

pricePerMonth.description: Mandatory for pricing model “Monthly”, otherwise optional. Gives the monthly product subscription price. The associated currency also has to be provided (priceCurrencyCode)

priceCurrencyCode.description: Mandatory for pricing model “Monthly” or “OneTime”, otherwise optional. Gives currency associated with the price/monthly price. For valid currency codes see application drop down.

shipments.description: Can be used to specify the shipment types that a Data Provider is offering. These shipment types are used if a customer filters offerings for a distinct shipment type. It is particularly important it the DataProvider has not products listed yet as only the shipment types given here are used for the search.

productArtifacts.description:
Contains one or multiple views that should be contained in the data product
(only views can be selected, selection of tables is not possible).
If Shipment Type is “Direct” in combination with Lifecycle Status “Listed”,
this is a mandatory property. For all other Shipment Types the product artifacts have to be left out.
Provides a list of all artifacts that the given DataProduct relates to.
Currently only views can be used as source artifacts for a DataProduct.
The artifacts have to exist in the given source space.

termsOfUse.description: Written, unformatted description of the product's terms of use. Terms of use are shown and confirmed by the customer during product activation

sizeCategory.description: This parameter is an information on how many records can be approximately expected in the data set. It should give a rough size estimation also in terms of consumed data volume in the target space. S =  Less than 1.000 records, M =  Between 1.000 and 1.00.000 records, L =   Between 1.00.000 and 1.000.000, XL =  between 1.000.000 and 10.000.000 records, XXL =  between 10.000.000 records and 100.000.000 records, XXXL =  more than 100.000.000 records

contractType.description: Defines how access to the product is managed/granted. „OnRequest“ is the mandatory contract type for all products that have shipment type = “OpenSQL” or “ExternalDelivery”.          „Free“ - Customer can activate the product without any further authorization by the DataProvider. „LicenseKey“ - The customer will need a valid license to key to authorize the product activation. The DataProvider can create a license through the Data Sharing Cockpit and give a customer access through activation keys which he can generate for this license.

deliveryMode.description: Controls if processes are activated that allow pushing regular data updates to customers of the given product. “OneTime” The data set is static and the customer should not expect updates on the data. Still the customer can trigger a refresh of the data if this should be necessary for any reason. “Full” The data set is periodically refreshed. The refresh pattern is described by the <DeliveryPattern> and <DeliveryPatternDescription>.The <DeliveryMode> is only relevant if <ShipmentType> is “Direct”.

deliveryPattern.description: Provides information on the update pattern of the data. Additionally, more precise information (e.g. on the time of update or on the weekday etc.) can be given in the <DeliveryPatternDescription>.Mandatory for <DeliveryMode> = “Full”.

deliveryPatternDescription.description: Free text that provides additional information when exactly a customer can expect an update of the product data
deliveryPatternDescription.example: Data is delivery on the 2nd workday of a week at 7pm CET

assignedContexts.description: Contexts to which the given data product is assigned

tenant.description: ID of the tenant from which the product has been created. Usually this field should be left empty. It is intended to be used if a product should move from one tenant to another.

lifecycleStatus.description: Is used to define if a product is visible in the Data Marketplace and sets the status of what a customer can do with the product. "Draft" Can be used to create a product without being visible in the Data Marketplace yet. "Listed" Makes a product being visible in the Data Marketplace. "Delisted" Temporarily remove a product from the Data Marketplace (make it invisible). Customer it is still possible to publish data releases and customers with existing installations can still work with the product and update data. "Deactivated" Completely removes the product from the Data Marketplace. Customers can't update data or re-install the product. Alternatively use the "changeLifecyceStatus" API to change the status. To finally delete a product use the "delete" method

enum: South Georgia & The South Sandwich Islands

name.description: The technical name of a DWC view

dataFilter.description: A filter that selects the row that should be shipped with the product (valid HANA where-clause).

columns.description:
Selects artifact columns which should be contained in the data product.
If none are specified, all columns are included.

name.description: File name of the document (including extension)

blobData.description:
BlobData Base64 encoded.
Must match the acceptable mime types.

name.description: File name of the image (including extension)

description.description: Descriptive subtitle of the image. Is shown with the image in the product page

blobData.description:
BlobData Base64 encoded.
Must match the acceptable mime types.

name.description: File name of the image (including extension)

blobData.description:
BlobData Base64 encoded.
Must match the acceptable mime types.

SampleDataObject.description:
Sample Data has to be provided in JSON format. The format of the data file is { \"data\":[{}, {}] }. In order to include sample data into the JSON-structure of the upload API make sure that

- All quotes are preceded by a backslash as escape character
- You don’t include any CR/LF; CR, LF in the sample data. It has to be one continuous line.

Example for a working JSON file (use word functionality to show (not) included CR’s):

{\"data\": [{\"Entity\": \"Afghanistan\",\"Code\": \"AFG\",\"Year\": \"1961\",\"Crops - Barley - 44 - Production - 5510 - tonnes\": \"378000\"},{\"Entity\": \"Afghanistan\",\"Code\": \"AFG\",\"Year\": \"1962\",\"Crops - Barley - 44 - Production - 5510 - tonnes\": \"378000\"},{\"Entity\": \"Zimbabwe\",\"Code\": \"ZWE\",\"Year\": \"2018\",\"Crops - Barley - 44 - Production - 5510 - tonnes\": \"55854\"}]}
SampleDataObject.title: SampleDataObject

blobData.description:
BlobData Base64 encoded.
Must match the acceptable mime types.

role.description: The role the provider has in that context.

status.description: The current status of the context.

scope.description: List of product UUIDs to be included in the license scope.

ContextType_POST_PUT_PATCH_DELETE.description: String values for the type of the corresponding context that can be managed by a provider using API.

ContextType_GET.description: String values for the type of the corresponding context that can be accessed by a provider.

ContextRole.description: The role the provider has in that context.

ContextStatus.description: The current status of the context.

Params_Provider_Identifier_Path.name: identifier
Params_Provider_Identifier_Path.description: The {identifier} can be used in two ways":" provide the UUID of the data provider or the content aggregator (a content aggregator is a data provider as well); or, provide the UUID of a content aggregator and a contentAggregatorProviderID separated by a colon.

Params_Product_Identifier_Path.name: productIdentifier
Params_Product_Identifier_Path.description: The productIdentifier identifies a data product. If the parameter providerUUID represents a data provider a dataProviderProductID (technical name) is required here. If the providerUUID represents a content aggregator, a contentAggregatorProductID (technical name)  is required.
example: Sample Product Name of a Provider

Params_State.name: state
Params_State.description: Holds the state. Either used or unused.

externalDocs.description: For more information, see the documentation of the CLI, which is based on those API's and provides example json's for some requests.
